market_summary: S&P 500 gained +0.9% for the week ending May 1, 2026, closing at a record 7,230.12 — its sixth consecutive weekly gain. Nasdaq gained +1.1% to 25,114.44, also a record. Strong Q1 earnings season was the primary driver: AMZN AWS grew 28% (15-quarter high), Google Cloud +28%, advertising sector strong. FOMC held at 3.50-3.75% on April 28-29 but with an unusually dovish 8-4 vote — one dissent for 25bps cut, the most divisions since October 1992. GLD pulled back to $423.18, now only 0.76% above the $420 hard trim trigger. NVDA fell from $208.27 to $198.45 (-4.8%) despite the broader market rally, pulling back from a recent higher peak.
macro_themes: 1. IRAN/HORMUZ (DOMINANT): Strait of Hormuz remains under dual blockade — US blockading Iranian ports, Iran restricting commercial transit. Ship transits collapsed 95% (130/day to 6/day). IEA confirmed largest supply disruption in global oil market history. GLD 4-week invalidation clock has NOT started — no durable resolution in sight. 2. INFLATION: March 2026 CPI +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM, primarily energy-driven (gasoline +18.9% YoY). April 2026 CPI due May 12 — expected to peak near 4% before declining if Hormuz resolves. Core CPI +2.6% YoY. 3. FED: FOMC held at 3.50-3.75% on April 28-29. Dovish surprise: 8-4 vote with one dissent (Governor Miran) for 25bps cut — most dissents since 1992. Next FOMC June 16-17; U03A stage-down plan now more relevant. 4. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: Hyperscalers committed $600B+ in 2026 AI capex. AMZN confirmed $200B FY2026 total capex. AWS 28% growth (15-quarter high). NVDA Vera Rubin in full production. NVDA Q1 FY2027 earnings May 20 is next major catalyst. 5. EARNINGS SEASON: Q1 2026 broadly strong — AMZN massive beat (EPS $2.78 vs $1.64 expected, AWS +28%). MA mixed — beat on EPS/revenue but cross-border volume 13% missed 14% thesis threshold; April cross-border decelerated to 9%. 6. SECTOR ROTATION: Tech and Nasdaq at all-time highs. Energy, Materials, Industrials led YTD. Tech momentum leader but cooling as AI capex ROI payoff questioned.
portfolio_health: Grade: B (78/100). Portfolio NAV $4,321.08, down -0.69% week-over-week vs S&P 500 +0.9% = -1.59pp underperformance. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +3.10%. Underperformance driven by NVDA pullback (-4.8%), GLD decline (-2.3%), and MA weakness, partially offset by CSPX gains (+1.6%) and AMZN resilience post-earnings. May 3 cash hard deadline met: CSPX $142.09 deployment executed at April 28 open (confirmed by position growth 1.9989 to 2.1843 shares). Cash reduced from $367.09 to $187.14 (4.33% of NAV). Critical alerts: GLD at $423.18 is only $3.18 (0.76%) above the $420 daily close trim trigger — most urgent risk. NVDA earnings May 20 is the highest-conviction near-term catalyst. MA cross-border missed threshold and April trend deteriorated to 9%. WHT structure near-optimal: 5 of 9 holdings at 0% WHT; three WHT-exposed residuals (MO, NLY, SPY) total $6.20 and should be exited.

positions.rationale: Core broad-market anchor at 39.42% of NAV. IE-domiciled accumulating structure is structurally optimal for a Lebanese resident. $142.09 deployment executed at April 28 open confirmed (position grew from 1.9989 to 2.1843 shares). S&P 500 at record 7,230 with 6 consecutive weekly gains. No new deployment needed at all-time highs; hold and deploy only on weakness or from rebalancing proceeds. Add $75 from this week's deployable cash to maintain anchor weight.

Q1 beat comprehensively: AWS +28% (15-quarter high), EPS $2.78 vs $1.64 expected, advertising +24%, chips exceeded $20B annual run rate. All ADD criteria technically met. However, $200B FY2026 capex and FCF collapse to $1.2B TTM are watchpoints. At current portfolio size, pre-NVDA-earnings add is a better capital deployment than extending AMZN further. Thesis: STRONGER.

WATCH — CRITICAL PROXIMITY TO $420 TRIGGER. GLD at $423.18 is only $3.18 (0.76%) above the hard $420 daily close trim trigger. Core macro thesis intact: Hormuz dual blockade (95% transit collapse), inflation elevated, central bank demand ~850 tonnes 2026 forecast. GLD 4-week invalidation clock NOT started. IF any daily close below $420: immediately trim 25% (~$106) into CSPX. No proactive reduction today given Hormuz macro support. Thesis: WEAKER (proximity to trigger).

positions.wht_exposure: 30% WHT on dividend (annualized <$5 at current size, immaterial)
positions.rationale: Q1 cross-border volume +13% missed the 14% thesis threshold. April trend deteriorated sharply to 9% cross-border growth; travel cross-border dropped from 8% to 2% YoY — Middle East conflict structurally suppressing highest-margin category. BVNK stablecoin acquisition ($1.5B) introduces capital allocation uncertainty. Reduce by ~$75 to reflect lowered conviction. Proceeds fund NVDA pre-earnings add. Thesis flag: WEAKER. Size change: -$75.
Residual $0.62 position with no thesis, no conviction, and 30% WHT exposure on a high-yield tobacco stock. Exit immediately. Proceeds to cash buffer.
Residual REIT position of $5.00 with no thesis on file and 30% WHT on distributions. No conviction basis for holding. Exit immediately. Proceeds to cash buffer.

Highest-conviction pre-earnings add ahead of confirmed May 20 Q1 FY2027 earnings. At $198.45, NVDA trades 23% below analyst consensus targets (~$268) and ~6.5% below recent ATH. Vera Rubin in full production, $1T+ backlog intact, hyperscaler $600B+ AI capex for 2026 confirmed. HBM shortage risk (KeyBanc -25% Rubin units) is a production timing risk, not demand invalidation. Add $150 (approx 0.756 shares) funded by MA reduction + U03A stage-down + deployable cash. Thesis: STRONGEST. Size change: +$150.
Residual $0.58 US-domiciled S&P 500 ETF — structurally inferior to CSPX for a Lebanese resident (30% WHT on distributions). No reason to hold alongside CSPX. Exit immediately.

First stage-down step per the active plan. FOMC April 28-29 delivered an unusually dovish 8-4 vote (one dissent for 25bps cut) — most divisions since 1992. Rate direction is clearly toward cuts; June 16-17 FOMC is next stage-down trigger review. Trim $75 (~0.70 shares) to reduce from 13.92% to ~12.2% of NAV. YTM 3.67% still above the 3.0% invalidation trigger. Proceeds fund NVDA pre-earnings add. Thesis: SAME. Size change: -$75.

rebalancing: Conviction ranking this week: NVDA (STRONGEST, pre-May 20 earnings) > CSPX (STRONGER, record highs) > AMZN (STRONGER, Q1 beat) > U03A (SAME, stage-down active) > GLD (WEAKER, $420 proximity) > MA (WEAKER, cross-border miss + April decel). Rebalancing actions: (1) Reduce MA by ~$75 — cross-border 13% missed 14% threshold, April decel to 9% is a material forward signal. (2) Trim U03A by ~$75 — FOMC 8-4 dovish vote confirms June rate cut risk, accelerates stage-down timeline. (3) Exit residual WHT-exposed positions MO, NLY, SPY (~$6.20 total) — no thesis, pure structural cleanup. (4) Add NVDA ~$150 pre-May 20 earnings — highest-conviction catalyst in near-term. (5) Add CSPX ~$75 from deployable cash — broad-market anchor at record levels. GLD holds unconditionally unless $420 daily close trigger fires.
cash_strategy: Cash reduced from $367.09 to $187.14 (4.33% of NAV) this week, confirming April 28 $142.09 CSPX deployment. Post-rebalancing: $187.14 current + $75 MA reduction + $75 U03A trim + $6.20 residual exits = ~$343.34 gross proceeds; minus $150 NVDA add and $75 CSPX add = ~$118 estimated post-trade cash. Target post-trade cash: ~$68 above $50 minimum buffer. This lean cash level is appropriate given two near-term binary events: GLD $420 trigger (can fire any day) and NVDA May 20 earnings. If NVDA disappoints, add to CSPX. If GLD trigger fires, trim proceeds go to CSPX immediately.

new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom is the highest-conviction new-position candidate. AI custom silicon dominance via Google TPU and Meta AI accelerator partnerships through 2029-2031. Q1 FY2026: revenue +29% YoY, AI revenue +106% YoY, $73B AI backlog targeting $100B in AI chips by 2027. Stock up +26% in 30 days. Q2 guidance: +47% YoY revenue growth. Strong Buy from 30 analysts, average target $443. Zero effective WHT (negligible dividend). Complementary to NVDA (custom XPUs vs standard GPUs). NAV GATE: $5,000 — current NAV $4,321 is $679 below threshold. Do NOT buy yet. Re-evaluate once NAV clears $5,000.

action_items: 1. CRITICAL — GLD $420 trigger (DAILY MONITOR): GLD at $423.18, only $3.18 (0.76%) above hard trim trigger. If any daily close below $420: immediately trim 25% of GLD (~$106) and redeploy into CSPX. Can fire at any time.
2. URGENT — Reduce MA by ~$75 (sell ~0.151 shares at market): Cross-border 13% missed 14% threshold; April decel to 9%; travel cross-border from 8% to 2%. Thesis: WEAKER. Proceeds fund NVDA add.
3. URGENT — Trim U03A by ~$75 (sell ~0.70 shares at market): FOMC 8-4 dovish vote confirms stage-down acceleration. First stage-down step per active plan. Proceeds fund NVDA add.
4. IMPORTANT — Exit MO ($0.62), NLY ($5.00), SPY ($0.58) at market: No thesis, 30% WHT exposure, residual cleanup. Execute this week.

5. IMPORTANT — Add NVDA ~$150 (buy ~0.756 shares, before May 20 earnings): Funded by MA reduction + U03A trim + deployable cash. Trades 23% below analyst consensus targets. Vera Rubin full production, $78.8B Q1 revenue consensus. CONFIRMED earnings date May 20 (resolves prior May 20 vs May 27 discrepancy).
6. IMPORTANT — Add CSPX ~$75: Deploy remaining free cash into broad-market anchor at record S&P 500 levels.

7. CALENDAR — NVDA May 20 Q1 FY2027 earnings: Consensus $78.8B revenue / $1.75 EPS. Watch: Vera Rubin ramp update, HBM supply constraint commentary, hyperscaler demand guidance. Date now confirmed May 20.
8. CALENDAR — April CPI May 12: Expected ~4% YoY peak. If higher-than-expected: GLD bullish, equities risk-off (use as NVDA/CSPX add opportunity). If lower-than-expected: U03A stage-down accelerates.
9. WATCH — AVGO NAV gate: Current NAV $4,321 needs +$679 to clear $5,000 threshold. Stock up +26% in 30 days with AI revenue +106% YoY. Re-evaluate for $300 initial position once NAV clears gate.
10. MONITOR — MA cross-border trend: If Q2 2026 earnings (late July) show cross-border below 10% again, escalate from WATCH to DETERIORATING and consider full exit.
